channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: These lifestyle changes may help you live longer after a cancer diagnosis
item.description: Adopting certain healthy habits — and cutting out a couple detrimental ones — can improve your outcomes.

item.title: Trump’s payout fund on shaky ground as Republicans push for retreat
item.description: The Justice Department said it would obey a court order pausing the nearly $1.8 billion fund, potentially signaling a broader pullback.

item.title: Spencer Pratt strikes a chord in frustrated Los Angeles
item.description: At a campaign event, the insurgent candidate taps into voter frustration.

item.title: Scott Pelley confronts new ‘60 Minutes’ boss in fiery meeting: ‘We don’t trust you’
item.description: The veteran correspondent demanded answers on fired staff and the new executive producer’s qualifications for the job.

item.title: Pentagon designates press office a classified space, further limiting media access
item.description: The change creates a new barrier compared with previous administrations, under which the office was an open room where reporters could stop by the desks of military public affairs officials without escorts.

item.title: Iran says it is breaking off talks to end war after U.S. and Israeli strikes
item.description: Negotiations were suspended because of Israel’s escalating military attacks in Lebanon and U.S. strikes, an Iranian official told The Post.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: A groom with cold feet is reluctant to walk away
item.description: Carolyn Hax: A groom with cold feet is reluctant to walk away

item.title: In the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential field, who’s leading? Here’s our analysis.
item.description: No candidate has officially jumped in, but governors, senators and former contenders are already shaping the race.

item.title: Loan rules would gut aid for college students whose majors yield low-paying jobs
item.description: Religious colleges, trade schools and others are lobbying to change proposed federal regulations that would deny loans for programs that don’t lead to higher salaries.

item.title: Trump betrayed farmers. Now real signs of anger show.
item.description: Hurt by the war in Iran and war on trade, will farmers still vote red?

item.title: Big words in today’s On the Record
item.description: Big words in today’s On the Record

item.title: Iran strikes. Presidential contenders. Kennedy Center. Trump fund. Cancer hope. Anxiety tips. Picket fences. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: What to know about the vagus nerve — and what the internet gets wrong about it
item.description: This vast network of about 200,000 nerve fibers supports physical and mental well-being in multiple ways, and activating it can help relieve our stress. But experts caution against social media hype about it.

item.title: Trump’s name may come off the Kennedy Center, but he could still destroy it, our columnist writes
item.description: The saga surrounding the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts took another turn with a judge’s ruling against the Trump administration last week. Columnist Philip Kennicott says the decision, in itself, is unlikely to save the institution.

item.title: Blue states want to tax payouts from Trump’s $1.8 billion fund — at 100 percent
item.description: Legislators in Democratic-led states are crafting bills, and Democratic candidates are rallying behind the tactic. The Trump administration has also drawn condemnation from some Republicans over the settlement fund for people who claim they were wrongly investigated by the government.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Mother jumps in the middle of couple’s decision to sleep apart
item.description: The couple sleep apart because it’s practical, but boyfriend’s mother is ready to set her son up on dates.

item.title: Still need to finish this weekend’s On the Record? We’ve got you.
item.description: Play now to finish out your score for the week.

item.title: Pancreatic cancer drug results open new era for fighting highly lethal disease
item.description: The highly anticipated results of a clinical trial for the pill, daraxonrasib, have been released, giving doctors hope for treating the stubbornly lethal disease. The drug kept patients alive for twice as long as those on regular chemotherapy.

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now

item.title: Desire for privacy is slowly killing this symbol of the American Dream
item.description: A white picket fence in the front yard once signified middle-class neighborliness. But hardly anyone buys them anymore, as more buyers seek hulking vinyl privacy fences instead.

item.title: Trump’s Kennedy Center plans were blocked by a judge. What happens next?
item.description: A federal judge’s ruling has thrown the Washington institution’s immediate future into a state of uncertainty.

item.title: Can’t do a European vacation? Visit these 10 European-inspired U.S. cities.
item.description: With high airfares and limited schedules, American travelers can still get a feel for Europe this summer even if they decide to stay closer to home. Towns and communities across the U.S. have preserved the traditions and culture of their immigrant ancestors.

item.title: Trump to headline Great American State Fair with a rally next month after several music acts cancel
item.description: Trump’s advisers worked quickly to organize a rally, set for June 24, as many performers slated for the concert series to be held this summer on the National Mall dropped out.

item.title: 10 therapist-approved ways to reduce anxiety fast
item.description: These techniques can calm your mind and body when anxious thoughts strike.

item.title: After hearing Paul McCartney on SNL, I thought he should hang it up — but I was wrong, our columnist writes
item.description: Geoff Edgers says it takes a Beatle to show us how to age — and how to refuse to fake it.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: There are no Supreme Court vacancies, but some judges are acting like there might be
item.description: Speculation is swirling over potential departures at the high court, and firebrand conservative judges might be using flashy rulings to audition for the president.

item.title: Judge probes whether deal creating Trump’s $1.8 billion payout fund constitutes fraud
item.description: A federal judge, acting on a rarely used judicial authority to investigate misconduct against the court, ordered lawyers for President Donald Trump to respond to allegations that an unusual deal to settle a lawsuit against the IRS constituted an act of fraud.

item.title: ‘The Four Seasons’ and ‘Couples Therapy’ crack open marriage
item.description: Hell is other people. Specifically the other person you decided to marry.

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explosion sets the stage for Elon Musk’s dominance of space
item.description: The destruction of Jeff Bezos's New Glenn rocket clouds NASA’s lunar timeline and deepens reliance on SpaceX, as the U.S. races China back to the moon.

item.title: Putin is cornered and lashing out
item.description: With Russian drones hitting Romania, now is not the time for Washington to up pressure on NATO allies, the Editorial Board writes.

item.title: Judge orders Kennedy Center to remove Trump’s name from building and halt plans for two-year closure
item.description: The ruling throws the Kennedy Center’s near future into uncertainty, blocking leaders from shutting down the performing arts venue after they have canceled most performances in anticipation of the closure.

item.title: Pentagon recruiting troops to watch White House UFC fights, memos show
item.description: Internal messages reviewed by The Washington Post stipulate that military personnel must pay their own ways and meet strict physical requirements to be eligible.

item.title: Over half of Great American State Fair performers drop out of event on National Mall over politics, threats
item.description: Bret Michaels and Martina McBride are the latest artists to pull out of the summerlong event organized by Freedom 250.

item.title: America is walking a tightrope to the ferocious conflict to come
item.description: Staying ahead of China requires pioneering technology — and stopping Chinese AI from conquering the world.

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Justice Department’s $1.8 billion payout fund
item.description: A federal judge in Virginia said the Trump administration may not transfer the money or process claims until at least June 12.

item.title: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record
item.description: Take today’s news quiz and complete your point total for the week.

item.title: Five killed, dozens injured in crash involving bus and six other vehicles on Interstate 95 in Stafford County, Virginia
item.description: The crash occurred around 2:35 a.m. where traffic was slowing ahead of a work zone, according to Virginia State Police.

item.title: A court in Kenya blocks U.S. plan to quarantine Americans exposed to Ebola at makeshift hospital
item.description:
The court, citing a threat to life, issued its ruling on the day U.S. officials said the facility would begin operating.
It has capacity for up to 50 patients potentially exposed during the growing Ebola outbreak, which is centered in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: Iran talks. Trump stocks. 2028 contenders. Rocket explosion. GLP-1 drugs. Spelling Bee. WWII mystery. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on launchpad during testing ahead of satellite mission
item.description: “We experienced an anomaly during today’s hotfire test,” the company said on social media, adding that all personnel had been accounted for. Blue Origin was preparing to send the reusable rocket to space for a mission delivering satellites into low Earth orbit.

item.title: Shrey Parikh, a 14-year-old from California, is the winner of the 2026 Scripps National Spelling Bee
item.description: Judges determined that Shrey spelled 32 words correctly during the spell-off, beating 12-year-old Ishaan Gupta’s 25 words.

item.title: The finals of the Scripps National Spelling Bee are underway with nine finalists. Follow our coverage.
item.description: The winner will take home a slew of prizes, including $50,000, a commemorative medal, the Scripps Cup, as well as $2,500 and a reference library from Merriam-Webster.

item.title: Dell inks $9.7 billion Pentagon contract after Trump acquires stock, praises company
item.description: Ethics experts say the president’s recent investments in the tech company create the appearance of a conflict of interest. Trump has repeatedly praised the company at public events, and government disclosure forms show that on Feb. 10 his portfolio acquired stock in Dell Technologies valued between $1 million and $5 million.

item.title: Ozempic may be reshaping the brain, scientists say
item.description: GLP-1 drugs may be rewiring circuits involved not only in appetite but in emotion, desire and beyond.

item.title: Trump has devastated Iran’s regime. Don’t give it a lifeline now.
item.description: Trump has devastated Iran’s regime. Don’t give it a lifeline now.

item.title: DOJ probes Democratic-allied nonprofit that helped fund columnist E. Jean Carroll’s lawsuit against Trump
item.description: The investigation centers on statements Carroll made during a deposition in her civil lawsuit against President Donald Trump, in which a jury found that he had sexually assaulted and defamed her.

item.title: As data centers boom, Virginians breathe the exhaust of 10,000 diesel generators
item.description: Pollution from generators at data centers could cause respiratory symptoms and deaths in the region, an analysis for The Washington Post found.

item.title: As data centers boom, Virginians breathe the exhaust of 10,000 diesel generators
item.description: Pollution from generators at data centers could cause respiratory symptoms and deaths in the region, an analysis for The Washington Post found.

item.title: Trump’s approval plunges among his White working-class base
item.description: In a striking shift, White voters without college degrees who voted to reelect Trump by a huge margin are now net-negative on his job approval.
